perturbation.original_text: Service Provider may use the Services by itself or provide similar services to any other third party within the area where Service Recipient operates or other areas.
perturbation.changed_text: Service Provider may provide similar services to any other third party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the text 'within the area where Service Recipient operates or other areas' grants Service Provider the right to directly compete with the Service Recipient in the same geographical area. Under China's Anti-Monopoly Law, such arrangements are scrutinized if they lead to unfair competition or harm to the smaller party (Service Recipient). Omitting geographic limitations introduces legal risk under Chinese competition law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Anti-Monopoly Law of the People's Republic of China, Articles 17-19
perturbation.law_citation: Anti-Monopoly Law of the People's Republic of China, Arts. 17-19
perturbation.law_explanation: Articles 17-19 address monopolistic agreements and practices that restrict competition. By removing the geographic limitation, the Service Provider could leverage its resources to stifle Service Recipient's market share, potentially violating these provisions if it leads to an abuse of a dominant market position. The modified text removes a safeguard that could prevent such a scenario, creating legal risk.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Letter of the Ministry of Finance on Submitting the Consolidated Ministerial Rules and Other Policies and Measures That Suit China's Accession to the World Trade Organization [Effective] 财政部关于报送适应加入世贸组织进程清理部门规章和其他政策措施情况的函 [现行有效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Ministries , Ministry of Finance Date issued： 07-24-2002 Effective date: 07-24-2002 Level of Authority: Departmental Regulatory Documents Area of Law: Legislative Affairs Letter of the Ministry of Finance on Submitting the Consolidated Ministerial Rules and Other Policies and Measures That Suit China's Accession to the World Trade Organization (Letter No.45 [2002] of the Ministry of Finance) According to the requirements of the Letter on Submitted Consolidated Ministerial Rules and Other Policies and Measures That Suit China's Accession to the World Trade Organization (Letter No.196 [2002]) of the Legal Affairs Office of the State Council, the consolidation of the relevant ministerial rules and other policies and measures of the present Ministry that involve the WTO is hereby reported as follows: ．．．．．． 财政部关于报送适应加入世贸组织进程清理部门规章和其他政策措施情况的函 （财法函[2002]45号） 根据国务院法制办公室《关于报送适应加入世贸组织进程清理部门规章和其他政府》（国法函［２００２］１９６号）的要求，现将我部对涉及ＷＴＯ的有关部门规章和其他政策措施进行清理的情况报告如下： 近两年来，为适应我国加入世贸组织的需要，按照你办及外经贸部的统一部署和要求，我部涉及ＷＴＯ的有关部门规章和其他政策措施进行了认真清理，并积极开展有关文件的修改和废止工作。经过清理，拟由我部和我部牵头修改或废止的部分规章和其他政策措施共２８件。截止目前，已有１４个文件如期完成了修改或废止工作（其中５件已修改，９件已废止），其他１４个文件的修改或废止工作正在进行之中。具体情况见附表。 ．．．．．． Dear visitor,you are attempting to view a subscription-based section of lawinfochina.com. perturbation.justification: The omission of 'within the area where Service Recipient operates or other areas' directly contradicts principles of fair competition, especially under China's Anti-Monopoly Law, as explained. The original text provided a geographical limitation that could prevent unfair competition. The removed text now permits the Service Provider to directly compete with the Service Recipient. Law Snippet 1 touches upon adherence to the World Trade Organization standards, implying that monopolistic behaviors are not acceptable under Chinese trade commitments. This removal introduces a legal risk by potentially enabling the Service Provider to leverage its resources against the Service Recipient, potentially violating anti-monopoly provisions. Although Snippet 1 is not directly about anti-monopoly law, it implies that China should be compliant with international fair-trade practices, and that local anti-monopoly laws are of importance.
